title: Dryopteris obovata
taxonomy: Dryopteris obovata is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris and the phylum Pteridophyta. The species is further classified into the kingdom Plantae and the division Pteridophyta. This classification is based on its characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns.
uses: Dryopteris obovata has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asian countries, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain. It is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating infections. In addition, Dryopteris obovata has been used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

properties: Dryopteris obovata, also known as the Japanese wood fern, has been found to possess several therapeutic properties. intro: Dryopteris obovata, also known as the Japanese shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris obovata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the Japanese shield fern is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to make granules, pills, or ointments.
